---
title: "DApipe_HvGerm_Exp3_TD2"
author: "Guido Giordano"
date: "2023-06-22"
output: html_document
---

#This is the pipeline for data analysis for barley germination experiment 3, time dependent, 3 vareities, with unique peptide filtering. 
My feeling is that in the quantified proteins I obtain a bit tto many NA values. This can be due to the not perfect way in which we checked the oktoberfest and picked group FDR stuff. It should be done again but it not a priority. 

#Load packages
```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("Biostrings")
BiocManager::install("org.At.tair.db")

library(cinaR)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggbreak)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(kableExtra)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(mice)
library(reshape2)
library(data.table)
library(VennDiagram)
library(Biostrings)
library(pheatmap)
library(matrixStats)
library(tidyverse)


```

#Calculate the digestion efficiency. This is directly connected with the goodness of protein identification. 

```{r}
#Load evidence (TD) file 
ev_Hv3_TD_2 <- read.table("../../R_Import/Hv3_TD_2/evidence2_pgFDR_output_filtered.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
#Generate a bar plot from the column missed cleavages
ggplot(ev_Hv3_TD_2, aes(x = factor(Missed.cleavages))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#105CB6", color="black") +
  labs(title = "Tryptic digestion efficiency (Hv3_TD2)",
       x = "Missed Cleavages",
       y = "Count") +
 theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),  
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain")) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)))
ggsave("../../R_export/TrypDig_Hv3TD2.png")

```

#Calculate TmT labelling efficiency for the experiment. NOT DONE. 

```{r}

```

#Now we start with data analysis of our proteinGroup file. 
#Import and filter proteinGroups file from Max Quant output tables. 

```{r}
#Import
evidence2_pgFDR_output_filtered <- read.table("../../R_Import/Hv3_TD_2/evidence2_pgFDR_output_filtered.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
pG_Hv3_TD2<- read.table("../../R_Import/Hv3_TD_2/pkGroups_Hv3TD.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#Remove potential contaminants, reverse 
pG_Hv3_TD2_noRev <-pG_Hv3_TD2[pG_Hv3_TD2$Reverse != "+", ]
pG_Hv3_TD2_noRev_noCont <-pG_Hv3_TD2_noRev[pG_Hv3_TD2_noRev$Potential.contaminant != "+", ]
#Log2(x) transformation
pG_Hv3_TD2_log2 <- pG_Hv3_TD2 %>%
  mutate(
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100), 
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100 = log2(Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100))
    
#Median normalization. Calculate median for each column in Reporter.intensity.corrected.1/2/3/4/5/6.Hv3_TD2 and then subtract the median from each column in the original data frame. Now the preliminary filtering and rearrangement of our data is completed. 
cols_to_fix <- c("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100")

# Replace -Inf with NA in the specified columns
pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[cols_to_fix][pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[cols_to_fix] == -Inf] <- NA

# Replace 0 with NA in the specified columns
pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[cols_to_fix][pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[cols_to_fix] == 0] <- NA

# Calculate medians, excluding both NA and -Inf values
medians_Hv3_TS <- apply(pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[, cols_to_fix], 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)

# Subtract medians from the columns
pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[, cols_to_fix] <- pG_Hv3_TD2_log2[, cols_to_fix] - medians_Hv3_TS

```

#Histogram that shows data distribution in each sample. The bins are the protein groups. 

```{r}
histo_Hv3_TD_AA_0 <- ggplot(pG_Hv3_TD2_log2, aes(x = Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.7, fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "0hs_AA",
       x = "Reporter Intensity",
       y = "Frequency") +
 theme_classic() +
  xlim(-10,10) +
   theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),  
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),  # Set same font for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain")) +  # Set same font for legend text
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),  # Update hjust value
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)))

histo_Hv3_TD_AA_24 <-ggplot(pG_Hv3_TD2_log2, aes(x = Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.7, fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +

       x = "Reporter Intensity",

 theme_classic() +

        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain")) +  # Set same font for legend text
```

#Eventually median normalize with log10 for graphical representation. NOT DONE HERE.  
#Import and filter proteinGroups file

```{r}
#Import
pG_Hv3_TD<- read.table("../../R_Import/Hv3_TD/pGroups_Hv3_TD.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#Remove potential contaminants, reverse 
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev <-pG_Hv3_TD[pG_Hv3_TD$Reverse != "+", ]
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont <-pG_Hv3_TD_noRev[pG_Hv3_TD_noRev$Potential.contaminant != "+", ]
#Log2(x) transformation
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2 <- pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont %>%
  mutate(
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100), 
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100),
    Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100 = log10(Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100))
    
#Median normalization. Calculate median for each column in Reporter.intensity.corrected.1/2/3/4/5/6.Hv3_TD and then subtract the median from each column in the original data frame. Now the preliminary filtering and rearrangement of our data is completed. 
cols_to_fix <- c("Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD", 
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD",
                 "Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD")

# Replace -Inf with NA in the specified columns
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[cols_to_fix][pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[cols_to_fix] == -Inf] <- NA

# Replace 0 with NA in the specified columns
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[cols_to_fix][pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[cols_to_fix] == 0] <- NA

# Calculate medians, excluding both NA and -Inf values
medians_Hv3_TS <- apply(pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[, cols_to_fix], 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)

# Subtract medians from the columns
pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[, cols_to_fix] <- pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2[, cols_to_fix] - medians_Hv3_TS

```

#Histogram that shows data distribution in each sample. The bins are the protein groups. 

```{r}
histo_Hv3_TD_AA_0 <- ggplot(pG_Hv3_TD_noRev_noCont_log2, aes(x = Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.7, fill = "#105CB6", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "0hs_AA",
       x = "log10 Reporter Intensity",
       y = "Frequency") +
 theme_classic() +
  xlim(-5,5) +
   theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),  
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),  # Set same font for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain")) +  # Set same font for legend text

        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)))

ggsave("../../R_export/DatDis_Hv3_TD3.png",  dpi = 150, width = 10)




```


#Prepare the pGroups identified/quantified for further analysis. Here we mutate in a new column the first entry of each pG from the fasta header. This allow us to have a key to look up in our annotation file obtained with Orthofinder and to annotate our protein groups identified. Moreover, this allow us to perform subsequent analysis that can cope with just one entry at a time. This also means that in these analysis we will consider just the first entry, losing all the other proteins in the protein group. The other entries will be still available in the columns fasta headers and protein ID, so it will still be possibile to find those genes when we will perform the search of specific proteins of our interest 

```{r}
#Extract the first entry of protein groups that will be used for annotation and remove useless columns. 
pG_Hv3_TD2_log2 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_log2 %>% 
  mutate(First_pGroup = str_extract(Protein.IDs, "^[^;]+")) %>%
  mutate(First_pGroup = word(First_pGroup, 1, sep = " ")) %>%
  select(First_pGroup, Protein.IDs, Fasta.headers, Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100, Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100)


#Load my annotation files (each for specie and since they are similar give a suffix to merge them later )
BeatriceV3_Barke<- read_csv("../../R_Data/Orthologues/BeatriceV3_Barke.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) 
colnames(BeatriceV3_Barke) <- paste0(colnames(BeatriceV3_Barke), "_BK")
BeatriceV3_HordSpont<- read_csv("../../R_Data/Orthologues/BeatriceV3_HordeumSpontaneum.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
colnames(BeatriceV3_HordSpont) <- paste0(colnames(BeatriceV3_HordSpont), "_HS")
BeatriceV3_HOR21599<- read_csv("../../R_Data/Orthologues/BeatriceV3_HOR21599.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
colnames(BeatriceV3_HOR21599) <- paste0(colnames(BeatriceV3_HOR21599), "_21599")
#Annotation of Protein groups identified with Orthologues file obtained with Orthofinder for each specie separately 
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK<-merge(pG_Hv3_TD2_log2, BeatriceV3_Barke, by.x = "First_pGroup", by.y = "Barke_Pg_BK", all.x =TRUE)
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS<-merge(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK, BeatriceV3_HordSpont, by.x = "First_pGroup", by.y = "HorSpont_OF_HS", all.x =TRUE)
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599<- merge(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS, BeatriceV3_HOR21599, by.x = "First_pGroup", by.y = "HOR21599_21599", all.x =TRUE)
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 %>%
  select(-c(19, 21, 23, 31, 39, 48, 55))

pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 19] <- paste(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 19], pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 94], sep = ",")
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 20] <- paste(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 20], pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 56], sep = ",")
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 21] <- paste(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 21], pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 58], pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 95], sep = ",")
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 57] <- paste(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 57], pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 93], sep = ",")
for (i in 22:55) {
  pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, i] <- paste(
    pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, i],
    pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, i + 37],
    pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, i + 74],
    sep = ","
  )
}
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 <- apply(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599, MARGIN = c(1, 2), function(x) {
  gsub(",?NA", "", x)
})

# Remove leading commas from the entire data frame
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 <- gsub("^,", "", pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599)

#remove all the columns that we dont need
pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 <- as.data.frame(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599)

pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 %>%
  select (c(1:18, 57, 19:55))
colnames(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599) <- sub("_BK$", "", colnames(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599))
colnames(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599)[19] <- sub("_HS$", "", colnames(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599)[19])

write.csv(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599, "../../R_Data/Hv3_TD2/pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

#Bar plot with proteins identified and unique. Number of proteins identified and  unique is identical as compared to Perseus! 
#With TmT efficiency very high, all the peptides are labelled and quantified. Thus we can say that all proteins identified are also quantified and does not make sense to separate the two things.
#In this case, the unique proteins proteins are considered inside each variety. This allows me to identify the qualitative differences between each time point in each variety. Then you can compare the unique proteins in each variety and see if they are the same or not. 
#Filtering for unique peptide, means that the peptide has to be identified at least one time in all the three varieties but it can be that is not identified in all the time points of all the varieties 

```{r}
#Determine the proteins quantified  in each time point 
filt_1_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_2_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.2.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_3_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.3.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_4_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.4.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_5_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.5.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_6_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.6.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_7_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.7.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_8_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.8.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_9_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.9.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_10_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.10.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_11_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.11.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_12_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.12.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_13_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.13.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_14_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.14.Hv3_TD_100)]
filt_15_Hv3_TD_100 <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup[!is.infinite(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100) & !is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599$Reporter.intensity.corrected.15.Hv3_TD_100)]

#Determine the proteins unique in each time point for each variety. 
uniqueP_1 <- setdiff(filt_1_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_2_Hv3_TD_100, filt_3_Hv3_TD_100, filt_4_Hv3_TD_100, filt_5_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_2 <- setdiff(filt_2_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_1_Hv3_TD_100, filt_3_Hv3_TD_100, filt_4_Hv3_TD_100, filt_5_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_3 <- setdiff(filt_3_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_1_Hv3_TD_100, filt_2_Hv3_TD_100, filt_4_Hv3_TD_100, filt_5_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_4 <- setdiff(filt_4_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_1_Hv3_TD_100, filt_2_Hv3_TD_100, filt_3_Hv3_TD_100, filt_5_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_5 <- setdiff(filt_5_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_1_Hv3_TD_100, filt_2_Hv3_TD_100, filt_3_Hv3_TD_100, filt_4_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_6 <- setdiff(filt_6_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_7_Hv3_TD_100, filt_8_Hv3_TD_100, filt_9_Hv3_TD_100, filt_10_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_7 <- setdiff(filt_7_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_6_Hv3_TD_100, filt_8_Hv3_TD_100, filt_9_Hv3_TD_100, filt_10_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_8 <- setdiff(filt_8_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_6_Hv3_TD_100, filt_7_Hv3_TD_100, filt_9_Hv3_TD_100, filt_10_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_9 <- setdiff(filt_9_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_6_Hv3_TD_100, filt_7_Hv3_TD_100, filt_8_Hv3_TD_100, filt_10_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_10 <- setdiff(filt_10_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_6_Hv3_TD_100, filt_7_Hv3_TD_100, filt_8_Hv3_TD_100, filt_9_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_11 <- setdiff(filt_11_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_12_Hv3_TD_100, filt_13_Hv3_TD_100, filt_14_Hv3_TD_100, filt_15_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_12 <- setdiff(filt_12_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_11_Hv3_TD_100, filt_13_Hv3_TD_100, filt_14_Hv3_TD_100, filt_15_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_13 <- setdiff(filt_13_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_11_Hv3_TD_100, filt_12_Hv3_TD_100, filt_14_Hv3_TD_100, filt_15_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_14 <- setdiff(filt_14_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_11_Hv3_TD_100, filt_12_Hv3_TD_100, filt_13_Hv3_TD_100, filt_15_Hv3_TD_100))
uniqueP_15 <- setdiff(filt_15_Hv3_TD_100, c(filt_11_Hv3_TD_100, filt_12_Hv3_TD_100, filt_13_Hv3_TD_100, filt_14_Hv3_TD_100))

# Calculate total identifications
filtered_data_total <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[rowSums(!is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 4:18])) > 0, ]
filtered_data_AA <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[rowSums(!is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 4:8])) > 0, ]
filtered_data_HS <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[rowSums(!is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 9:13])) > 0, ]
filtered_data_BK <- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[rowSums(!is.na(pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599[, 14:18])) > 0, ]
# Create a Venn diagram showing the coverage
Hv3TS2_Venn_coverage <- venn.diagram(
  x = list(Barke_Proteome = 1:45999, Barke_proteome_covered = 1:7138),
  fill = c("lavender", "skyblue4"), 
  print.mode = "percent", 
  cat.pos = c(0,0),
  cat.default.pos = "outer",
  offset = 0.7, 
  rotation.degree = 90, 
  cat.cex = 1.5,
  cex = 1.5, 
  filename = "../../R_export/Hv3_TD2/Hv3TS2_Venn_coverage.png")

#Create a bar plot with proteins quantified and unique
#Create a dataset with the proper information
p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD <- data.frame(Time = c("0hs_AA", "24hs_AA", "48hs_AA", "72hs_AA", "96hs_AA", "0hs_HS", "24hs_HS", "48hs_HS", "72hs_HS", "96hs_HS", "0hs_BK", "24hs_BK", "48hs_BK", "72hs_BK", "96hs_BK"),
                           Proteins_quantified = c(6598, 6491, 7323, 7464, 7419, 6757, 6878, 7281, 7210, 7296, 6849, 7207, 7069, 7266, 7345),
                           Unique_Proteins = c(5, 4, 9, 44, 46, 13, 4, 70, 17 ,31, 9, 20, 1, 9, 70))
# Convert the "Time" variable to a factor with the levels in the desired order
p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD$Time <- factor(p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD$Time, levels = c("0hs_AA", "24hs_AA", "48hs_AA", "72hs_AA", "96hs_AA", "0hs_HS", "24hs_HS", "48hs_HS", "72hs_HS", "96hs_HS","0hs_BK", "24hs_BK", "48hs_BK", "72hs_BK", "96hs_BK"))

# Add a new variable "Group" to represent AA, HS, and BK. This helps me to divide the plot in 3 parts 
p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD$Group <- factor(
  ifelse(grepl("AA", p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD$Time), "AA",
         ifelse(grepl("HS", p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD$Time), "HS", "BK")),
  levels = c("AA", "HS", "BK")
)

# Plot the data with ggplot2

ggplot(p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD, aes(x = Time)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Proteins_quantified, fill = "Proteins quantified"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.7, color="black") +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Unique_Proteins, fill = "Unique proteins"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width=0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~Group, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +  # Separate by groups
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#A80000", "#105CB6"), 
                    labels = c("Quantified proteins", "Unique proteins")) +
  labs(title = "Proteins quantified and unique in each variety",
       x = " ", 
       y = "Protein groups", 
       fill="") +
  scale_y_cut(breaks=100) +  
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs", "0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs", "0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs")) +  # Specify x-axis labels
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, family = "", face = "plain")) +  
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)),
        axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.4, margin = margin(t = 4)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 4)), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, vjust = 0.9, hjust = 1))

ggsave("../../R_export/Hv3_TD2/pQuant_Unique_Hv3_TD.png", dpi = 150, width = 7.5)

#Make a plot with only identifications 
ggplot(p_Qau_Uni_Hv3_TD, aes(x = Time)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Proteins_quantified, fill = "Proteins quantified"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.7,  color="black", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Group, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +  # Separate by groups
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#A80000")) +
  labs(title = "Proteins quantified in each variety",
       x = " ", 
       y = "Protein groups", 
       fill="") +
  scale_y_cut(breaks=100) +  
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs", "0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs", "0hs", "24hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs")) +  # Specify x-axis labels
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, family = "", face = "plain"),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, family = "", face = "plain"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, family = "", face = "plain")) +  
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)),
        axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.4, margin = margin(t = 4)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 4)), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, vjust = 0.9, hjust = 1))
ggsave("../../../R_export/pQuant_Hv3_TD_34.png", dpi = 250, width = 7, height = 4)

```

```{r}
# Create a Venn diagram for each species ----
HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_AA <- venn.diagram(
  x = list(AA_proteome = 1:44456, AA_proteome_covered = 1:7296),
  fill = c("lavender", "skyblue4"), 
  print.mode = "percent", 
  cat.pos = c(0,0),
  cat.default.pos = "text",
  offset = 0.7, 
  rotation.degree = 90, 
  filename = "../../R_export/HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_AA.png")

HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_BK <- venn.diagram(
  x = list(BK_proteome = 1:45999, BK_proteome_covered = 1:7419),
  fill = c("lavender", "skyblue4"), 
  print.mode = "percent", 
  cat.pos = c(0,0),
  cat.default.pos = "text",
  offset = 0.7, 
  rotation.degree = 90, 
  filename = "../../R_export/HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_BK.png")

HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_HS <- venn.diagram(
  x = list(HS_proteome = 1:36395, HS_proteome_covered = 1:7494),
  fill = c("lavender", "skyblue4"), 
  print.mode = "percent", 
  cat.pos = c(0,0),
  cat.default.pos = "outer",
  offset = 0.7, 
  rotation.degree = 90, 
  cat.cex = 1.5,
  cex = 1.5,  
  filename = "../../R_export/HvG3_Hv3TD_Venn_HS.png")
``` 

#Unique protein does not have to be investigated but rather used as a quality control. HERE NOT DONE. 
```{r}
#Generate a table with all the unique proteins for each time point. A useful table is created (slice just proteins that I need in my presentation)

Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_0hs_AA <- pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599[pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup %in% uniqueP_1, ] %>%
  select("First_pGroup", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%
  kable("html", escape = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, html_font = "Helvetica") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3B83BD", color = "white")%>%
  add_header_above(c("Unique proteins 0hs AA" = 6))%>%
  column_spec(1:6, bold = TRUE)%>%
  as_image(file = "../../R_export/Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_0hs_AA.png", width = 700, height = 400)

Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_0hs_AA_useful <- pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599[pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup %in% uniqueP_1, ] %>%
  select("Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%
  slice(2) %>%
  kable("html", escape = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3B83BD", color = "white")%>%
  add_header_above(c("Unique proteins 0hs_AA" = 5))%>%
  column_spec(1:5, bold = TRUE)%>%
  as_image(file = "../../R_export/Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_0hs_AA_useful.png", width = 800, height = 400)

Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_48hs_AA <- pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599[pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup %in% uniqueP_3, ] %>%

  select("First_pGroup", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%

  select("Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%
  slice(6,10,12,3,19,44, 24,33) %>%

Table_UniqueP_Hv3_TD_96hs_AA_useF <- pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599[pG_main_TD3_anno_BK_HS_21599$First_pGroup %in% uniqueP_5, ] %>%
  select("Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%
  slice(10,11,15) %>%
  kable("html", escape = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3B83BD", color = "white")%>%

```
#UpSet plot showing protein combinations between samples. This plot is not saved because i dont really know the function of this plot. 

```{r}
#Load packages
library(UpSetR)

#Luckily, we previously obtained vectors for each sample so we just have to create an adequate list to input in UpSetR fuction 
lt_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS<-list(filt_1_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS, filt_2_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS, filt_3_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS, filt_4_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS, filt_5_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS, filt_6_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS)
names(lt_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS) <- c("0hs", "24hs", "36hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs")

#Now we can use the function Upset 

UpSet_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS<-upset(fromList(lt_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS), sets= c("0hs", "24hs", "36hs", "48hs", "72hs", "96hs"), nsets=6, nintersects=6, mainbar.y.label = "Protein intersections", sets.x.label = "Proteins identified", mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45))
UpSet_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_HS

```

#Obtain the most abundant proteins in each sample. NOT DONE. Just checked the first couple of samples. 

```{r}
#All most abundant. 

MAP_0hs_Hv3_TD_BK<- pG_Hv3_TD2_anno_BK_HS_21599 %>% 
  arrange(desc(Reporter.intensity.corrected.1.Hv3_TD_100))%>%
  head(10)%>%
  select("First_pGroup", "Barke_Pg_ProteinNames", "Wheat_ProteinNames", "Maize_ProteinNames", "Rice_ProteinNames", "Arabidopsis_ProteinNames")%>%
  kable("html", escape = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3B83BD", color = "white")%>%
  add_header_above(c("MAP_0hs_BK" = 6))%>%
  column_spec(1:5, bold = TRUE)%>%
  as_image(file = "../../R_export/Hv3_TD2/MAP_0hs_HvGerm_Exp3_TD_BK.png", width = 1000, height = 400)

  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#3B83BD", color = "white")%>%
  add_header_above(c("MAP_96hs_BK" = 5))%>%
  column_spec(1:5, bold = TRUE)%>%
  as_image(file = "../../R_export/MAP_96hs_HvGerm_Hv3_TD_BK.png", width = 800, height = 400)
```
